heading: Funding for development and adoption of measurably secure semiconductors and measurably secure semiconductors supply chains
label: 4655 Funding for development and adoption of measurably secure semiconductors and measurably secure semiconductors supply chains
notes: Amendments2023—Subsecs. (c), (d). redesignated subsec. (d) as (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which required the Secretary of State to report to Congress annually on the implementation of this section.Pub. L. 118–31
amendments: Amendments2023—Subsecs. (c), (d). redesignated subsec. (d) as (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which required the Secretary of State to report to Congress annually on the implementation of this section.Pub. L. 118–31

children.heading: Establishment of fund
children.label: 1 Establishment of fund
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury is authorized to establish a trust fund, to be known as the “Multilateral Semiconductors Security Fund” (in this section referred to as the “Fund”), consisting of any appropriated funds credited to the Fund for such purpose.

children.heading: Reporting requirement
children.label: 2 Reporting requirement
children.text: If the Fund authorized under subsection (a)(1) is not established, 180 days after , and annually thereafter until such Fund is established, the Secretary of the Treasury, in coordination with the Secretary of State, shall provide, in writing, to the appropriate committees of Congress a rationale for not establishing the Fund.January 1, 2021

children.heading: Investment of amounts
children.label: A Investment of amounts
children.text: If the Fund authorized under subsection (a)(1) is established, the Secretary of the Treasury shall invest such portion of the Fund as is not required to meet current withdrawals in interest-bearing obligations of the United States or in obligations guaranteed as to both principal and interest by the United States.

children.heading: Interest and proceeds
children.label: B Interest and proceeds
children.text: The interest on, and the proceeds from the sale or redemption of, any obligations held in the Fund shall be credited to and form a part of the Fund.

children.label: i
children.text: to provide funding through the common funding mechanism described in subsection (b)(1) to support the development and adoption of measurably secure semiconductors and measurably secure semiconductors supply chains; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to otherwise carry out this section.

children.heading: Availability contingent on international arrangement or agreement
children.label: B Availability contingent on international arrangement or agreement

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Amounts in the Fund shall be available to the Secretary of State, subject to appropriation, on and after the date on which the Secretary of State enters into an arrangement or agreement with the governments of countries that are partners of the United States to participate in the common funding mechanism under paragraph (1) of subsection (b).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: ii Consultation
children.text: Before entering into an arrangement or agreement as described clause (i), the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of Commerce, shall ensure any partner government maintains export control licensing policies on semiconductor technology substantively equivalent to the United States with respect to restrictions on such exports to the People’s Republic of China.

children.heading: Common funding mechanism for development and adoption of measurably secure semiconductors and measurably secure semiconductors supply chains
children.label: b Common funding mechanism for development and adoption of measurably secure semiconductors and measurably secure semiconductors supply chains

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of Commerce, the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of Energy, and the Director of National Intelligence, is authorized to establish a common funding mechanism, in coordination with foreign partners, that uses amounts from the Fund to support the development and adoption of secure semiconductors and secure semiconductors supply chains, including for use in research and development collaborations among partner countries participating in the common funding mechanism. In establishing and sustaining a common funding mechanism, the Secretary of State should leverage United States funding in order to secure contributions and commitments from trusted foreign partners, including cost sharing and other cooperative measures leading to the development and adoption of secure semiconductors and secure microelectronic supply chains.

children.label: A
children.text: establish transparency requirements for any subsidies or other financial benefits (including revenue foregone) provided to semiconductors firms located in or outside such countries;

children.label: i
children.text: are not participating in the common funding mechanism; and

children.label: ii
children.text: do not meet transparency requirements established under subparagraph (A);

children.label: C
children.text: promote harmonized treatment of semiconductors and verification processes for items being exported to a country considered a national security risk by a country participating in the common funding mechanism;

children.label: D
children.text: establish consistent policies and common external policies to address nonmarket economies as the behavior of such countries pertains to semiconductors;

children.label: E
children.text: align policies on supply chain integrity and semiconductors security, including with respect to protection and enforcement of intellectual property rights; and

children.label: F
children.text: promote harmonized foreign direct investment screening measures and export control policies with respect to semiconductors to align with national, multilateral, and plurilateral security priorities.

children.heading: Notifications to be provided by the Fund
children.label: c Notifications to be provided by the Fund

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 15 days prior to the Fund making a financial commitment associated with the provision of expenditures under subsection (a)(4)(A) in an amount in excess of $1,000,000, the Secretary of State shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress report in writing that contains the information required by paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of each such expenditure;

children.label: B
children.text: an identification of the recipient or beneficiary; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the project or activity and the purpose to be achieved by an expenditure of the Fund.

children.heading: Arrangements or agreements
children.label: 3 Arrangements or agreements
children.text: The Secretary of State shall notify the appropriate committees of Congress not later than 30 days after entering into a new bilateral or multilateral arrangement or agreement described in subsection (a)(4)(B).
